tubulars.name: 2.375" J55 Tubing
tubulars.description: API Specification 5CT grade J55 light-duty production tubing.
limitations: Low corrosion resistance in sour gas
Low collapse rating

tubulars.name: 2.875" L80 Tubing
tubulars.description: API Specification 5CT grade L80 production tubing with controlled hardness for sour service.
limitations: Not suitable for high CO2 with high watercut without inhibition
applications: Standard production wells with mild H2S

tubulars.name: 3.500" T95 Tubing
tubulars.description: High-strength sour service production tubing designed for severe H2S environments with strict QC.
limitations: Higher cost
Susceptible to severe CO2 corrosion in wet gas without inhibitors

tubulars.name: 4.500" P110 Tubing
tubulars.description: High-strength standard production tubing for deep, high-pressure wells.
limitations: Not resistant to H2S (SSC hazard)
Strict temperature limits under stress

tubulars.name: 5.500" Q125 Casing
tubulars.description: Ultra-high strength casing designed for high pressure deep wells.
advantages: High collapse resistance for deep reservoirs
limitations: No H2S resistance (very brittle in sour environment)

tubulars.name: 7.000" P110 Casing
tubulars.description: High-strength intermediate and production casing for deep wells.
advantages: Great balance of tensile strength and price
limitations: High susceptibility to SSC in H2S environments

tubulars.name: 9.625" L80 Casing
tubulars.description: Intermediate casing designed for sweet to mild sour environments.
advantages: Controlled yield strength for mild sour service
limitations: Not for high H2S concentrations
Medium pressure limit

tubulars.name: 13.375" J55 Casing
tubulars.description: Surface casing used to isolate shallow water zones and support subsequent strings.
limitations: Low collapse threshold
No acid/H2S resistance

tubulars.name: 20.000" K55 Casing
tubulars.description: Large diameter surface or conductor casing for structural support.
limitations: Low burst and collapse rating
Heavy weight per unit length

tubulars.name: 2.875" E75 Drill Pipe
tubulars.description: Standard light-weight drill pipe commonly used in slim-hole drilling and workovers.
advantages: Excellent flexibility in high dogleg severities
limitations: Low torsional limit compared to high grades

tubulars.name: 3.500" G105 Drill Pipe
tubulars.description: High strength drill pipe commonly used in medium-depth directional wells.
limitations: Limited sour service compatibility

tubulars.name: 4.500" X95 Drill Pipe
tubulars.description: Medium-high strength drill pipe for standard deep well drilling.
advantages: Good fatigue life in abrasive wells
limitations: Lower tensile limit than S135

tubulars.name: 5.000" S135 Drill Pipe
tubulars.description: Ultra-high strength drill pipe for deep vertical and extended reach wells.
limitations: Highly susceptible to sulfide stress cracking in H2S
High cost

tubulars.name: 5.500" S135 Drill Pipe
tubulars.description: Ultra-heavy high capacity drill pipe for maximum torque and flow rate.
advantages: Lower pressure drop due to large ID
limitations: Very heavy, requires high capacity rigs
Extremely stiff

tubulars.name: 4.500" L80 Casing
tubulars.description: API Specification 5CT grade L80 casing, commonly used for production casing strings in deep oil and gas wells.
limitations: Requires chemical inhibition in sweet CO2 wells with high water cut

tubulars.name: 7.000" L80 Casing
tubulars.description: API Specification 5CT grade L80 casing for intermediate or production casing strings in deep reservoirs.
limitations: Susceptible to general weight loss corrosion in wet CO2

tubulars.name: 9.625" P110 Casing
tubulars.description: High-strength API Spec 5CT grade P110 casing for deep, high-pressure intermediate or production casing applications.
advantages: Very high tensile and yield strength
High collapse resistance for salt zone squeezing
limitations: Not suitable for sour service (susceptible to SSC)

tubulars.name: 13.375" N80 Casing
tubulars.description: Medium-strength API Spec 5CT grade N80 casing, typically used for surface or intermediate casing strings.
limitations: Highly susceptible to SSC in sour service
Lower collapse rating
applications: Intermediate casing strings in shallow to medium depth wells

tubulars.name: 2.875" J55 Tubing
tubulars.description: Standard API Specification 5CT grade J55 tubing for light-duty production and injection wells.
limitations: Vulnerable to sour corrosion
Low tensile capacity

tubulars.name: 3.500" L80 Tubing
tubulars.description: API Specification 5CT grade L80 production tubing for high-pressure or sour well completions.
limitations: Requires chemical inhibition for CO2 and high water cuts
applications: Sour gas and oil production wells

tubulars.name: 5.000" G105 Drill Pipe
tubulars.description: High-strength API Spec 5DP grade G105 drill pipe designed for heavy-duty drilling operations in medium to deep wells.
limitations: Susceptible to Sulfide Stress Cracking in low-pH sour fluids

threads.name: EUE (External Upset End)
threads.description: Standard API connection with external upset on tubing ends for standard oilfield completions.
advantages: High joint strength due to upset
limitations: Upset increases outer diameter
Not gas-tight (interference seal only)
threads.running_notes: Use API modified thread compound. Clean pins and boxes thoroughly before stabbing.
threads.gas_tight_suitability: Not rated for gas pressure, interference seal only.
threads.running_notes_ru: Использовать резьбовую смазку стандарта API. Тщательно очистить ниппель и муфту перед свинчиванием.

threads.name: BTC (Buttress Thread Connection)
threads.description: API standard casing connection with buttress profile, offering high tensile strength.
advantages: Good resistance to bending and compression
limitations: Not gas-tight under high pressure gas (requires liquid/interference seal)
applications: Intermediate and production casing strings in sweet wells
threads.running_notes: Align accurately to prevent cross-threading. Control final makeup torque carefully.
threads.gas_tight_suitability: Not rated for gas pressure, interference seal only.
threads.running_notes_ru: Выравнивать соосно во избежание повреждения витков. Контролировать конечный момент свинчивания.

threads.name: ОТТМ (ГОСТ 632)
threads.description: Standard Russian trapezoidal thread connection for casing strings.
advantages: Простой монтаж на буровых установках РФ
limitations: Не обеспечивает герметичность по газу высокого давления
applications: Строительство вертикальных и наклонно-направленных скважин на суше
threads.running_notes: Использовать смазку Р-402. Контролировать натяг по торцу муфты.
threads.gas_tight_suitability: Not rated for gas pressure, interference seal only.
threads.running_notes_ru: Использовать смазку Р-402. Контролировать натяг по торцу муфты.

threads.name: ОТТГ (ГОСТ 632)
threads.description: Russian premium casing connection with metal-to-metal seal and torque shoulder.
advantages: Стойкость к высокому внутреннему давлению газа
limitations: Чувствительность к загрязнению резьбы перед свинчиванием
applications: Газовые и газоконденсатные скважины, глубокие скважины
threads.running_notes: Тщательно очистить узел уплотнения. Избегать соударения при спуске.
threads.gas_tight_suitability: Not rated for gas pressure, interference seal only.
threads.running_notes_ru: Тщательно очистить узел уплотнения. Избегать соударения при спуске.

threads.name: VAM TOP
threads.description: Premium gas-tight threaded connection for casing and tubing with metal-to-metal seal.
limitations: Expensive
Requires specialized field service representatives for running
threads.running_notes: Use VAM-approved thread compound. Torque-turn monitoring is highly recommended.
threads.torque_envelope: CAL-IV qualified, max torque up to 8,500 ft-lbs.
threads.compression_tension_behavior: Compression load capability equals 100% of pipe body yield.
threads.galling_risks: High risk in CRA materials, moderate risk in carbon steels.
threads.compatible_lubricants: API modified compound, metal-free premium greases.
threads.field_assembly_notes: Inspect metal seal area for scratches before stabbing.
threads.connection_type_ru: Европейское премиальное соединение с уплотнением металл-металл
threads.running_notes_ru: Использовать смазку, одобренную VAM. Рекомендуется использовать прибор контроля момента-оборота.

threads.name: TenarisHydril Blue
threads.description: Premium gas-tight connection with double torque shoulder and high compression rating.
limitations: High premium price
High sensitivity to misalignment during stab
threads.running_notes: Clean thoroughly. Apply dope uniformly over the threads, avoiding excessive build-up on seal.
threads.compression_tension_behavior: Compression capability is 100% of pipe body.
threads.field_assembly_notes: Clean box carefully to remove shipping grease.
threads.running_notes_ru: Тщательно очистить. Наносить резьбовую смазку равномерно, избегая избытка на уплотнении.

threads.name: TMK UP (РФ Premium)
threads.description: Russian premium gas-tight connection family for tubing and casing with metal-to-metal seals.
limitations: Требует сертифицированных ключей для свинчивания
threads.running_notes: Использовать специализированный прибор для контроля момента-оборота.
threads.gas_tight_suitability: Not rated for gas pressure, interference seal only.
threads.running_notes_ru: Использовать специализированный прибор для контроля момента-оборота.

threads.name: TP-CQ (Китай Premium)
threads.description: Chinese premium connection with high torque capacity and metal-to-metal seal.
advantages: Excellent torque capacity for horizontal sections
limitations: Not widely used outside China/Asia projects
applications: Extended reach horizontal wells in China/Asia
threads.seal_type: Metal-to-metal radial seal with external shoulder
threads.running_notes: Verify torque gauges before execution. Avoid aggressive stabbing.
threads.gas_tight_suitability: Not rated for gas pressure, interference seal only.
threads.seal_type_ru: Радиальное уплотнение металл-металл с внешним упорным уступом
threads.running_notes_ru: Проверить манометры крутящего момента перед работой. Избегать резкой посадки труб.

threads.name: VAM 21 (Premium Casing & Tubing)
threads.description: Next-generation premium threaded and coupled connection featuring a unique thread design and 100% burst/collapse envelope rating.
chemicalComposition: Compatible with carbon steel, 13Cr, Super 13Cr, and high-nickel CRAs
advantages: 100% structural efficiency under combined tension and internal pressure
CAL IV validation according to ISO 13679
limitations: Requires certified VAM technicians for running
Critical makeup control necessary (torque-turn charts)
threads.compatibility: Carbon steels and high-nickel alloys, optimized for Dopeless running
why_selected: 100% rating under both burst and collapse makes casing design much safer
Eliminates makeup errors due to visual shoulder check
why_avoided: Higher connection cost compared to standard VAM TOP
Not necessary for standard shallow vertical wells
threads.gas_tight_suitability: Not rated for gas pressure, interference seal only.
threads.compatibility_ru: Углеродистые стали и сплавы с высоким содержанием никеля, оптимизированные для свинчивания без резьбовой смазки (Dopeless)

threads.name: TenarisHydril Wedge 563
threads.description: Premium threaded and coupled connection utilizing a dovetail wedge thread profile that provides high torque capability without a torque shoulder.
chemicalComposition: Compatible with carbon steels and high-strength metallurgies
advantages: Exceptional bending capacity (suitable for horizontal wells)
Does not require a torque shoulder to seal
limitations: High galling risk if run without correct thread dope
Difficult to clean and inspect in the field
threads.compatibility: High strength carbon steel grades (P110, Q125)
why_selected: Dovetail thread wedge action provides highest compression capacity in the industry
Highly resistant to jump-out in high-curvature wells
why_avoided: Risk of galling in high-alloy CRAs unless specialized dopeless versions are used
threads.gas_tight_suitability: Not rated for gas pressure, interference seal only.

threads.name: VAM HT (High Torque)
threads.description: Premium connection designed for high torque applications in drilling and casing strings.
threads.running_notes: Verify torque limit prior to makeup. Run slowly.
threads.torque_envelope: Max torque up to 12,000 ft-lbs depending on wall thickness.
threads.compression_tension_behavior: 100% compression rating matching pipe body yield.
threads.compatible_lubricants: API modified compound, metal-free thread dopes.
threads.typical_failures: Shoulder yielding due to excessive torque.
threads.running_notes_ru: Проверить ограничение крутящего момента перед свинчиванием. Спускать медленно.

threads.name: Tenaris Blue Dopeless
threads.description: Dry premium connection requiring no thread compound (dope-free) for environmental safety.
threads.running_notes: Do not apply dope. Ensure dry and clean surfaces. Protect from dust.
threads.torque_envelope: Symmetric envelope with solid shoulder feedback.
threads.compression_tension_behavior: Excellent compression performance; equal to standard Tenaris Blue.
threads.galling_risks: Low. Dry lubricant coating protects pins and boxes.
threads.running_recommendations: Keep thread protector on until final stabbing.
threads.field_assembly_notes: Dry clean only. Water/oil on threads can cause makeup errors.
threads.running_notes_ru: Не наносить резьбовую смазку. Обеспечить сухость и чистоту поверхностей. Защищать от пыли.

threads.name: FOX Premium Connection
threads.description: Premium casing/tubing thread designed with a unique pitch change to distribute load and reduce galling.
threads.torque_envelope: Broad envelope due to localized pitch design.
threads.galling_risks: Low. Pitch change minimizes contact pressure.
threads.running_recommendations: Run at low RPM to ensure even thread loading.
threads.field_assembly_notes: Visual check of shoulder alignment is critical.

threads.name: BEAR Premium Connection
threads.description: High-collapse premium casing thread optimized for thick-wall salt zone casings.
threads.galling_risks: Moderate. Heavy wall thickness requires good lubrication.
threads.field_assembly_notes: Ensure no mud enters pin or box.

threads.name: Atlas Bradford Premium
threads.description: Standard flushing joint premium connection with gas-tight seal for slim-hole completions.
threads.running_notes: Ideal for tight clearances. Run at low speed.
threads.torque_envelope: Lower torque limits due to slim joint design.
threads.compression_tension_behavior: Reduced tension capability compared to coupling joints.
threads.galling_risks: Moderate to High. Slim profile requires precise alignment.
threads.typical_failures: Slim box expansion, thread jump-out under high tension.
threads.running_notes_ru: Идеально для малых зазоров. Спускать на низкой скорости.

threads.name: Hydril PH6 Premium Tubing
threads.description: Two-step premium tubing connection with high tensile efficiency and gas-tight seal.
threads.running_notes: Two-step threads: slide together, then rotate. Do not cross-thread.
threads.galling_risks: Low. Two-step design limits initial thread contact.
threads.field_assembly_notes: Ensure double shoulder contacts are clean.
threads.typical_failures: Box splitting due to excessive torque.
threads.running_notes_ru: Двухступенчатая резьба: вставить коаксиально до упора, затем свинтить. Избегать перекоса.

threads.name: Hydril CS Premium Tubing
threads.description: Standard two-step premium tubing connection with flush joint profile for clearance wells.
threads.running_notes: Flush outer diameter. Stabbing must be perfectly vertical.
threads.torque_envelope: Low torque envelope due to flush wall thickness.
threads.compression_tension_behavior: Reduced tension capacity compared to PH6.
threads.galling_risks: Moderate. Flush joints have thinner sections.
threads.field_assembly_notes: Examine thin box area for damage.
threads.typical_failures: Thread strip-out under tension, box parting.
threads.running_notes_ru: Безмуфтовый наружный диаметр. Посадка должна быть строго вертикальной.

threads.name: eXtreme Torque (XT)
threads.description: Drill pipe premium connection designed for extreme torsional loads in directional and ERD wells.
threads.running_notes: Designed for rotation. Use premium drill pipe dope.
threads.gas_tight_suitability: Highly reliable fluid seal, not CAL-IV certified gas-tight.
threads.galling_risks: Low. Heavy tool joint geometry prevents galling.
threads.running_recommendations: Clean tool joint threads thoroughly after each trip.
threads.compatible_lubricants: Copper-based or zinc-based drill pipe dopes.
threads.typical_failures: Box swelling under severe drilling torque.
threads.seal_type_ru: Двойной упорный торец с уплотнением металл-металл
threads.running_notes_ru: Предназначен для вращения при бурении. Использовать премиальную смазку для бурильных труб.

elastomers.name: Viton (FKM / Фторкаучук)
elastomers.description: Fluorocarbon elastomer with exceptional temperature and chemical resistance in sweet and mildly sour environments.
limitations: Rapidly degrades in steam, amines, and high concentration H2S (vulcanization reversal)
elastomers.chemical_compatibility: Excellent with crude oil, HCl, aliphatic hydrocarbons; poor with amines/steam.
elastomers.rgd_resistance: Medium to High (depending on compounding structure).
elastomers.steam_resistance: Poor. Suffers rapid chemical reverse vulcanization.
elastomers.failure_mechanisms: Amine hardening, chemical degradation in steam/amines.
elastomers.storage_recommendations: Aged storage up to 10 years in sealed bags away from ozone.
elastomers.field_limitations: Do not run in injection/geothermal wells with steam.

elastomers.name: NBR (Nitrile / Бутадиен-нитрильный каучук)
elastomers.description: Standard oilfield elastomer with excellent physical strength and wear resistance in low-temperature oil service.
usedInEquipment: Stator linings for progressive cavity pumps
limitations: Poor resistance to ozone, polar solvents, H2S, and temperatures above 120°C
elastomers.chemical_compatibility: Good with hydrocarbons; poor with polar solvents, ozone, and hot water.
elastomers.failure_mechanisms: Ozone cracking, heat embrittlement, sour gas swelling.
elastomers.storage_recommendations: Max 5 years shelf life. Keep out of daylight.

elastomers.name: HNBR (Hydrogenated Nitrile / Гидрированный БНК)
elastomers.description: Hydrogenated nitrile elastomer showing high mechanical performance and better H2S resistance than NBR.
limitations: Moderate performance in hot steam and strong concentrated acids
elastomers.chemical_compatibility: Good with hydrocarbons; poor with polar solvents, ozone, and hot water.
elastomers.failure_mechanisms: Ozone cracking, heat embrittlement, sour gas swelling.
elastomers.storage_recommendations: Max 5 years shelf life. Keep out of daylight.

elastomers.name: Kalrez / FFKM (Перфторкаучук)
elastomers.description: Ultimate performance perfluoroelastomer with near-complete chemical inertia, resisting severe sour service.
limitations: Extremely expensive
Poor low-temperature elasticity
Low mechanical tear resistance
elastomers.chemical_compatibility: Near total chemical inertia. Resistant to acids, steam, amines, and H2S.
elastomers.failure_mechanisms: Dynamic extrusion due to low mechanical tear strength, low temperature leakage.
elastomers.field_limitations: Extremely expensive. Fails to seal at low temperatures (<-15°C).

elastomers.name: Teflon / PTFE (Фторопласт)
elastomers.description: Thermoplastic fluorpolymer with total chemical resistance. Used with elastomeric energizers.
limitations: Subject to cold flow (creep) under compression
Zero elasticity (requires spring/rubber energizer)
elastomers.chemical_compatibility: Total chemical resistance to all downhole fluids.
elastomers.failure_mechanisms: Cold flow (creep) deformation, lack of elastic memory.
elastomers.field_limitations: Cannot be used as primary elastic energizer (must be backed up with elastomer).

elastomers.name: AFLAS (FEPM / Фторкаучук)
elastomers.description: Unique fluoroelastomer resistant to a broad range of harsh chemicals, specifically designed for steam, amines, and sour environments.
elastomers.swelling_resistance: Excellent in amines, basic drilling muds, and steam. Poor in aromatic hydrocarbons, ketones, and chlorinated solvents.
typical_failures: Extrusion under high pressure if backup rings are absent
typical_applications: Packer seals in steam injection wells
why_selected: AFLAS is immune to amine-based corrosion inhibitors that destroy standard Viton (FKM)
why_avoided: Avoid in sub-zero polar environments due to loss of elasticity below -10C
Avoid in presence of low-aniline point mineral oils
sources: Asahi Glass Company AFLAS Tech Manual
limitations: Poor low-temperature flexibility
Poor resistance to aromatic solvents

elastomers.name: EPDM (Ethylene Propylene Diene Monomer)
elastomers.description: Standard oilfield elastomer designed for extreme hot water, steam, and polar chemical service, but completely incompatible with hydrocarbons.
elastomers.swelling_resistance: Excellent in water, steam, and polar solvents. Completely swells and disintegrates in crude oil, diesel, and hydrocarbons.
typical_failures: Catastrophic swelling and extrusion in oil/hydrocarbon service
typical_applications: Steam stimulation well packer elements (hydrocarbon-free zones)
Subsurface safety valve hydraulic seals (water-based fluid)
why_selected: Economic seal material for non-hydrocarbon geothermal systems
why_avoided: Strictly avoid in any oil, gas, or hydrocarbon mud contact; even trace oil causes rapid seal failure
limitations: Not compatible with petroleum-based oils, mineral oils, or hydrocarbon solvents

elastomers.name: PEEK (Polyetheretherketone / Полиэфирэфиркетон)
elastomers.description: High-performance thermoplastic polymer offering exceptional mechanical strength, wear resistance, and chemical compatibility at high temperatures.
elastomers.swelling_resistance: Immune to swelling. Absorption of wellbore chemicals is negligible.
typical_failures: Brittle cracking if subjected to extreme impact or bending at low temperatures
Creep deformation under continuous ultra-high loads at >200C
typical_applications: Anti-extrusion backup rings for elastomer O-rings
why_selected: Combines metal-like mechanical strength with total chemical inertness up to 260C
Standard anti-extrusion choice for HPHT O-ring seal packages
why_avoided: Avoid where flexibility is required (rigid polymer, cannot function as a dynamic seal by itself)
limitations: Very rigid (plastic, not a true elastomer)
High cost
Sensitive to concentrated sulfuric acid

elastomers.name: PU (Polyurethane / Полиуретан)
elastomers.description: Thermoplastic elastomer with extreme mechanical strength, high wear and abrasion resistance.
elastomers.chemical_compatibility: Good with crude oil, water, aliphatic hydrocarbons. Poor with polar solvents and acids.
elastomers.rgd_resistance: Excellent. Strong mechanical properties prevent gas decompression cracking.
elastomers.sour_service_suitability: Poor. Susceptible to amine and H2S degradation.
elastomers.steam_resistance: Very poor. Hydrolyzes rapidly in hot water or steam.
elastomers.acid_compatibility: Poor. Degrades in hydrochloric or organic stimulation acids.
elastomers.failure_mechanisms: Hydrolysis (reversion), chemical degradation by acids and H2S, thermal softening.
elastomers.storage_recommendations: Store in cool, dark, dry environment. Avoid moisture.
elastomers.field_limitations: Do not use in high-temperature or wet wells.
limitations: Poor high-temperature resistance
Susceptible to hydrolysis in hot water/steam

elastomers.name: XNBR (Carboxylated Nitrile / Карбоксилатный БНК)
elastomers.description: Carboxylated nitrile elastomer offering supreme abrasion and tear resistance, for high mechanical load applications.
elastomers.chemical_compatibility: Excellent with petroleum oils and water. Poor with highly polar solvents.
elastomers.rgd_resistance: High. Carboxylation increases physical density and resistance to RGD.
elastomers.sour_service_suitability: Moderate. Marginally better than standard NBR, but not for high H2S.
elastomers.acid_compatibility: Moderate. Resists trace concentrations of acid wash.
elastomers.failure_mechanisms: Thermal oxidation, cracking, wear in sour gas.
elastomers.storage_recommendations: Cool, dry space, away from UV and ozone.
elastomers.field_limitations: Not suitable for HPHT sour completions.
limitations: Poor resistance to polar solvents and ozone
Higher compression set than standard NBR

elastomers.name: Fluorosilicone (FVMQ / Фторсиликон)
elastomers.description: Specialized fluorinated silicone rubber offering outstanding low-temperature elasticity and fuel resistance, ideal for arctic surface equipment.
elastomers.chemical_compatibility: Good with petroleum oils and fuels. Completely incompatible with amines, ketones, and steam.
elastomers.rgd_resistance: Low. Susceptible to rapid gas decompression damage due to high gas permeability.
elastomers.sour_service_suitability: Low. Not recommended for sour service completions.
elastomers.steam_resistance: Poor. Suffers rapid hydrolysis in steam and hot water above 100°C.
elastomers.acid_compatibility: Moderate. Poor resistance to strong acids.
elastomers.failure_mechanisms: Physical tear, explosive decompression, ozone degradation.
elastomers.storage_recommendations: Store in cool, dry place, strictly away from direct UV radiation.
elastomers.field_limitations: Poor mechanical strength; avoid dynamic applications.
limitations: Poor physical strength (low tensile/tear)
Not recommended for dynamic friction applications

elastomers.name: Chemraz (FFKM / Перфторкаучук)
elastomers.description: Premium perfluoroelastomer featuring near-complete chemical inertness, excellent thermal stability, and superior explosive decompression resistance.
elastomers.chemical_compatibility: Almost universal resistance. Virtually unaffected by all oilfield chemicals.
elastomers.rgd_resistance: Excellent. Outstanding performance in high-pressure rapid gas decompression environments.
elastomers.sour_service_suitability: Outstanding. Highly recommended for severe H2S completions.
elastomers.steam_resistance: Excellent. Thermally stable in steam up to 300°C.
elastomers.acid_compatibility: Outstanding. Immune to acid stimulation fluids.
elastomers.failure_mechanisms: Extrusion under high pressure without backup rings, poor low-temperature hardening.
elastomers.storage_recommendations: Standard elastomer storage guidelines apply. Clean, dark, dry conditions.
limitations: Extremely high cost
Poor low-temperature flexibility for standard grades

elastomers.name: HNBR High-ACN
elastomers.description: Hydrogenated nitrile butadiene copolymer with high acrylonitrile content, offering maximum resistance to oil swelling and gas decompression.
elastomers.chemical_compatibility: Excellent with petroleum oils, H2S, and water. Poor with highly polar solvents.
elastomers.rgd_resistance: Outstanding. High ACN level provides density to resist explosive decompression.
elastomers.sour_service_suitability: Excellent. Highly suitable for moderate H2S gas and oil completions.
elastomers.steam_resistance: Good. Suitable for hot water and steam up to 150°C.
elastomers.acid_compatibility: Moderate. Good resistance to organic acids, poor to concentrated mineral acids.
elastomers.failure_mechanisms: Thermal aging, low-temperature cracking, ozone exposure.
elastomers.storage_recommendations: Keep in airtight containers away from UV light.
elastomers.field_limitations: Do not expose to temperatures below -20°C.
limitations: Poor low-temperature flexibility
Limited resistance to strong acids

brines.name: NaCl (Sodium Chloride / Хлорид натрия)
brines.description: Standard monovalent salt brine used for completion and workover.
chemicalCompatibility: Compatible with carbon steels and standard seals
limitations: Max density limit of 1.20 sg (10 ppg) at saturation
Corrosive in oxygenated environments

brines.name: CaCl₂ (Calcium Chloride / Хлорид кальция)
brines.description: Divalent salt brine used for higher density completion requirements.
chemicalCompatibility: Highly corrosive to carbon steel in presence of dissolved oxygen
advantages: Achieves density up to 1.39 sg (11.6 ppg)
limitations: Relatively high cost
Corrosive to packer metals without corrosion inhibitors

pt_reference.name: Fresh Water Gradient (Пресная вода)
pt_reference.description: Standard baseline hydrostatic fluid gradient calculations based on pure water density.
limitations: Does not account for temperature salinity variations

pt_reference.name: Salt Water Gradient (Соленая вода)
pt_reference.description: Standard hydrostatic gradient baseline for offshore and sea water applications.
limitations: Salinity variations in different seas

standards.name: Casing & Tubing Standard Mappings (ОКТ и НКТ)
standards.description: Cross-reference equivalency map for casing and tubing specifications.
limitations: Minor differences in testing standards may exist
standards.scope: Casing, tubing, pup joints, couplings, connectors and accessories for use in oil and gas wells. Covers grades H40 through Q125. Specifies mechanical properties, dimensional tolerances, testing, inspection, and marking.
standards.scope_ru: Обсадные и насосно-компрессорные трубы, переводники, муфты и фитинги для нефтегазовых скважин. Охватывает группы прочности H40–Q125. Регламентирует механические свойства, размеры, испытания, контроль и маркировку.

standards.name: Drill Pipe Specifications (Бурильные трубы)
standards.description: Equivalency standard mapping for steel drill pipes and tool joints.
advantages: Allows standard drill string integrity verification across countries
limitations: Differences in upset geometry standards
applications: Drill string design and load limit calculations
standards.scope: Drill pipe for use in rotary drilling operations. Covers grades E-75 through S-135. Specifies dimensions, mechanical properties, inspection, and marking for drill pipe body and tool joints.
standards.scope_ru: Бурильные трубы для роторного бурения. Охватывает группы прочности E-75–S-135. Регламентирует размеры, механические свойства, контроль и маркировку тела трубы и замков.

standards.name: API Spec 5CT / ISO 11960
standards.description: Specification for Casing and Tubing. Governs the mechanical, chemical, and dimensional requirements for steel pipes used in oil and gas wells.
advantages: Ensures strict quality control and dimensional uniformity
limitations: Does not cover premium connections testing (governed by ISO 13679)
Does not cover corrosion resistant alloys (covered by API 5CRA)
applications: Material specification for standard carbon steel casing and tubing procurement
why_selected: Essential foundation standard for casing and tubing design in oil and gas completions
why_avoided: Supplement with API 5CRA when using stainless steel or nickel alloys
standards.purpose: Defines technical delivery conditions for casing and tubing steel pipes, coupling stock, and accessory materials.
standards.scope: Primary standard for OCTG (oil country tubular goods). Specifies manufacturing, testing, and inspection requirements for casing and tubing used in wells. Grades: J55, K55, N80, L80, C90, T95, P110, Q125.
standards.critical_sections: Section 7: Chemical composition and hardness limits. Section 10: NDT inspection levels (SR1, SR2). Table C.1: Tensile and yield properties.
standards.cross_standard_mapping: API Spec 5CT ↔ ISO 11960 ↔ ГОСТ 31446 (fully harmonized for steel grades up to P110).
standards.scope_ru: Основной стандарт для НКТ и ОКТ (нефтепромысловые трубы). Определяет требования к производству, испытаниям и контролю обсадных и насосно-компрессорных труб. Группы: J55, K55, N80, L80, C90, T95, P110, Q125.

standards.name: NACE MR0175 / ISO 15156
standards.description: Materials for use in H2S-containing environments in oil and gas production. Defines strict rules to prevent sulfide stress cracking (SSC) and stress corrosion cracking (SCC).
advantages: Crucial standard for safety in sour oilfield operations
Prevents catastrophic failures due to hydrogen embrittlement
limitations: Does not specify mechanical design limits, only chemical/metallurgical thresholds for H2S service
applications: Material selection guidelines for all wells with H2S partial pressure > 0.05 psi
why_selected: Mandatory global standard for environmental safety and materials compliance in sour service completions
why_avoided: Not required for sweet wells where H2S is completely absent (reduces material cost significantly)
standards.purpose: Establishes requirements and recommendations for the selection and qualification of carbon steels, low-alloy steels, and CRAs in sour environments.
standards.scope: Materials for use in H2S-containing environments in oil and gas production. Defines requirements for metallic materials for sulfide stress cracking (SSC) resistance. Mandatory for sour service applications.
standards.critical_sections: Part 2, Clause 8: Hardness requirements for carbon steels (max 22 HRC). Part 3, Annex A: Environmental limits for CRAs (temperature vs. H2S limits).
standards.cross_standard_mapping: NACE MR0175 ↔ ISO 15156 ↔ ГОСТ Р 53678 (sour service compliance verification).
standards.scope_ru: Материалы для работы в средах, содержащих H2S, при добыче нефти и газа. Определяет требования к металлическим материалам по сульфидному растрескиванию под напряжением (ВРН/SSC). Обязателен для кислых сред.

standards.name: Connection Validation & Testing (Резьбовые соединения)
standards.description: Equivalency and performance testing requirements for casing and tubing connections.
advantages: Validates gas tightness under combined loads
limitations: CAL IV requires extreme testing envelopes and thermal cycling
standards.scope: Defines test procedures for casing and tubing connections. Classes CAL I through CAL IV specify tests for tension, compression, internal/external pressure, and thermal cycles.
standards.scope_ru: Регламентирует испытания обсадных и НКТ резьб. Классы от CAL I до CAL IV определяют тесты на растяжение, сжатие, внутреннее/внешнее давление и температурные циклы.

standards.name: Sour Service Materials & H2S Limits (Сероводородостойкость)
standards.description: Equivalency mapping for H2S service limitations and metallurgical properties.
advantages: Defines clear operational operational pH-H2S zones
limitations: Restricts maximum allowable material hardness to 22 HRC (for steels)
standards.scope: Specifies requirements and recommendations for the selection and qualification of carbon/low-alloy steels and CRAs for H2S-containing environments in oil and gas production.
standards.scope_ru: Устанавливает требования к выбору и квалификации углеродистых, низколегированных и коррозионностойких сплавов для эксплуатации в сероводородосодержащих средах нефтегазодобычи.

standards.name: Packers & Bridge Plugs Validation (Внутрискважинные пакеры)
standards.description: Equivalency map for downhole isolation packers and validation grades.
limitations: V0/V3 testing is extremely expensive and time-consuming
standards.scope: Provides requirements for packers and bridge plugs. Defines validation grades V6 (lowest, liquid test) to V0 (highest, gas test with thermal cycling and zero leakage).
standards.scope_ru: Устанавливает требования к пакерам и мостовым пробкам. Определяет уровни валидации от V6 (низший, гидравлический тест) до V0 (высший, газовый тест с термоциклированием и нулевой утечкой).

standards.name: Elastomers & Seals Qualification (Квалификация эластомеров)
standards.description: Validation testing and aging standards for non-metallic seals.
advantages: Ensures seal longevity in harsh chemical environments
limitations: Norsok M-710 autoclave testing does not simulate dynamic seal wear
standards.scope: Defines qualification procedures for non-metallic sealing materials, testing resistance to rapid gas decompression (RGD) and high-temperature chemical aging.
standards.scope_ru: Регламентирует квалификацию неметаллических уплотнений, включая стойкость к взрывной декомпрессии газа (RGD) и химическому старению при высоких температурах.

standards.name: Drill Stem Equipment Inspection (Инспекция бурильных труб)
standards.description: Wear classification and inspection mapping for drill stem components.
limitations: Different wear categorization between DS-1 and API
standards.scope: Establishes inspection requirements for used drill stem elements. Compares wear classes and defines procedures for electromagnetic and ultrasonic testing.
standards.scope_ru: Устанавливает требования к инспекции бурильного инструмента. Сравнивает классы износа и регламентирует методы неразрушающего контроля (ЭМК, УЗК).

acid_environments.name: Carbon Steel in HCl (Углеродистая сталь в HCl)
acid_environments.description: Corrosion rate mapping of Carbon Steel in Hydrochloric Acid (HCl) under stimulation conditions.
limitations: Rapid metal loss without organic amine inhibitors above 60°C

acid_environments.name: 13Cr Steel in Sour Gas (13Cr сталь в H₂S + CO₂)
acid_environments.description: Performance and corrosion envelopes for 13Cr Martensitic Stainless Steel in H2S and CO2 environments.
limitations: Susceptible to SSC at high H2S partial pressures and low pH
applications: Wells with high CO2 content and trace H2S concentrations

steel_grades.name: L80 Carbon Steel
steel_grades.description: API 5CT controlled hardness carbon steel grade designed for mild sour service (H2S) environments.
steel_grades.chemical_composition: Carbon Steel with Chrome and Moly additions, strictly controlled hardness (Max 23 HRC)
steel_grades.sour_service_suitability: Suitable for H2S service under NACE MR0175 / ISO 15156 limits
steel_grades.temperature_suitability: Stable down to -29C (-20F), suitable for deep hot wells up to 180C
steel_grades.corrosion_resistance: Resistant to Sulfide Stress Cracking (SSC). Poor CO2 resistance without corrosion inhibitors.
advantages: NACE MR0175 compliant for sour service
High yield strength compared to H40/J55
Excellent global availability and standard sizing
limitations: High corrosion rate in wet CO2 environments without chemical inhibition
Brittle failure risk if hardness exceeds 23 HRC
typical_applications: Production and injection wells with mild H2S
steel_grades.engineering_notes: Always verify hardness certification (maximum 23 HRC is mandatory for NACE compliance).

steel_grades.name: P110 Carbon Steel
steel_grades.description: High-strength API 5CT carbon steel grade for deep, high-pressure wells without H2S risks.
steel_grades.sour_service_suitability: NOT suitable for H2S service (susceptible to rapid Sulfide Stress Cracking)
steel_grades.corrosion_resistance: Standard carbon steel corrosion rate. High susceptibility to SSC.
advantages: Extremely high tensile and collapse capacity
Most cost-effective grade for deep sweet wells
limitations: Strictly forbidden in sour gas or sour oil wells due to catastrophic SSC risks
steel_grades.engineering_notes: Never use in sour wells. Ensure strict QA/QC to avoid accidental H2S exposure.

steel_grades.name: Q125 High-Strength Steel
steel_grades.description: Ultra-high strength API 5CT carbon steel grade designed for high-pressure deep wells. Requires special manufacturing controls.
steel_grades.chemical_composition: Quenched & Tempered Cr-Mo Alloy Steel with strict grain size requirements
steel_grades.sour_service_suitability: Generally NOT suitable for sour service, though highly controlled chemistry Q125 can be used in limited mild sour conditions with operator approval
steel_grades.temperature_suitability: Stable at deep downhole conditions up to 200C
steel_grades.corrosion_resistance: High strength makes it very brittle in presence of hydrogen or H2S
advantages: Ultimate load capability for deep reservoirs
High collapse resistance under heavy mud weight
limitations: High susceptibility to cracking under stress in sour environments
Very high cost and limited sourcing
steel_grades.engineering_notes: Manufacturing quality and heat treatment records must be fully traceable.

steel_grades.name: 13Cr Martensitic Stainless Steel
steel_grades.description: Martensitic stainless steel with 13% Chromium, providing excellent resistance to CO2 weight-loss corrosion.
steel_grades.sour_service_suitability: Limited. Suitable for trace H2S only (partial pressure H2S < 0.1 psi / 1.5 kPa) depending on pH.
steel_grades.temperature_suitability: Max temperature limit of 150C (302F) to prevent pitting in chloride environments
steel_grades.corrosion_resistance: Superb resistance to sweet CO2 corrosion. High pitting risk in presence of oxygen and high chlorides.
advantages: Eliminates the need for continuous downhole corrosion inhibitors in CO2 wells
High yield strength equivalent to L80
limitations: Extremely prone to galling during makeup (requires premium thread compounds and slow makeup speed)
High cost compared to L80 carbon steel
typical_applications: Water injection wells with dissolved CO2
steel_grades.engineering_notes: Always use specialized thread compounds (e.g. metal-free premium dopes) and slow RPM during makeup to prevent galling.

steel_grades.name: Super 13Cr (HP2-13Cr)
steel_grades.description: Enhanced martensitic stainless steel with low Carbon, Nickel, and Molybdenum additions, offering higher strength and improved H2S resistance over standard 13Cr.
steel_grades.sour_service_suitability: Intermediate. Suitable for H2S partial pressures up to 1.5 psi (10 kPa) depending on pH and temperature.
steel_grades.temperature_suitability: Extended limit up to 180C (356F) in mild sour conditions
steel_grades.corrosion_resistance: Excellent CO2 resistance, superior pitting and SSC resistance compared to standard 13Cr.
advantages: Extends the envelope of 13Cr to higher temperatures and mild sour service
limitations: Significant cost premium over standard 13Cr
Requires premium thread connections and careful handling to prevent damage
typical_applications: Deep hot wells with CO2 and mild H2S
steel_grades.engineering_notes: Check specific manufacturer limits for pH vs Cl- concentration vs H2S partial pressure envelopes.

steel_grades.name: Inconel 718 (Nickel-Based CRA)
steel_grades.description: Precipitation-hardenable nickel-chromium alloy containing significant amounts of iron, niobium, and molybdenum, designed for extreme high-strength and severe sour service.
steel_grades.sour_service_suitability: Fully suitable for severe sour service (100% H2S) with no limits under NACE MR0175 up to high temperatures.
steel_grades.temperature_suitability: Exceptional stability from cryogenic temperatures up to 350C+ downhole
steel_grades.corrosion_resistance: Virtually immune to CO2 corrosion, chloride stress corrosion cracking, and H2S sulfide stress cracking.
used_in_equipment: Subsurface safety valve springs and housings
advantages: Ultimate mechanical strength combined with ultimate corrosion resistance
Immunity to SSC/SCC under extreme loading
limitations: Extremely high cost (up to 10-15x carbon steel)
Very difficult to machine and fabricate
steel_grades.engineering_notes: Verify material certification to ensure proper solution annealing and precipitation hardening treatment.

steel_grades.name: 25Cr Super Duplex Steel
steel_grades.description: Premium 25% Chromium duplex stainless steel with high molybdenum and nitrogen content for highly corrosive environments.
steel_grades.sour_service_suitability: Suitable for sour service up to 3.0 psi H2S partial pressure (NACE MR0175 limits)
typical_applications: Highly corrosive downhole environments with high chlorides and moderate H2S
steel_grades.mechanical_properties: Yield: 80,000 - 125,000 psi (552 - 862 MPa), Tensile: Min 110,000 psi (758 MPa). Hardness: Max 32 HRC.
steel_grades.h2s_compatibility: Excellent. Resistant to higher H2S partial pressures than 22Cr Duplex (up to 3.0 psi).
steel_grades.co2_compatibility: Exceptional. Complete resistance to sweet weight-loss corrosion up to 230°C.
steel_grades.collapse_yield_considerations: Excellent load capacity for deep corrosive HPHT wells.
steel_grades.galling_tendency: Very High. Demands premium thread coatings, slow stabbing, and clean joints.
steel_grades.corrosion_mechanisms: Sulfide Stress Cracking if NACE limits are exceeded in extreme environments.
steel_grades.field_limitations: Strict NACE limits apply for H2S partial pressures at high temperatures.
steel_grades.common_failure_modes: Running galling, stress corrosion cracking under extreme acidizing jobs.

steel_grades.name: 28Cr Austenitic Stainless Steel
steel_grades.description: Highly alloyed austenitic stainless steel with superb resistance to sour environments containing chlorides and hydrogen sulfide.
advantages: Excellent resistance to stress corrosion cracking in sour chloride media
Superb general corrosion resistance in wet acid gas
limitations: Relatively high cost due to chromium/nickel content
Lower yield strength in annealed state compared to cold-worked Duplex grades
typical_applications: Tubulars for corrosive sour wells with high temperature and watercut
steel_grades.sour_service_suitability: Highly compatible (excellent resistance to SSC and SCC)
steel_grades.corrosion_resistance: Superior pitting resistance in hot brine and sour environments containing sulfur compounds.
why_selected: Bridges the gap between Super 13Cr and expensive nickel-based alloys in warm, moderately sour wells
why_avoided: Avoid in extreme chloride/H2S environments above 230C where nickel-based Inconel 718 is mandatory
Avoid if simple 13Cr provides sufficient envelope (cost optimization)

steel_grades.name: 22Cr Duplex Stainless Steel
steel_grades.description: Austenitic-ferritic duplex stainless steel with 22% Chromium, providing high mechanical strength and excellent resistance to CO2/H2S.
steel_grades.mechanical_properties: Yield: 65,000 - 110,000 psi (448 - 758 MPa), Tensile: Min 90,000 psi (621 MPa). Hardness: Max 28 HRC.
steel_grades.h2s_compatibility: Good. Suitable for sour service under NACE MR0175 limit envelopes (pH vs. H2S partial pressure).
steel_grades.co2_compatibility: Outstanding. Almost immune to CO2 weight-loss corrosion up to 200°C.
steel_grades.chloride_resistance: High. Excellent resistance to pitting and chloride stress corrosion cracking.
steel_grades.collapse_yield_considerations: Very high yield ratings in cold-worked versions (e.g. 110 ksi yield grades).
steel_grades.galling_tendency: High galling risk during running. Requires specialized metal-free lubricants and controlled torque-turn monitoring.
steel_grades.corrosion_mechanisms: Local pitting in severe low-pH chloride solutions under trace oxygen.
steel_grades.field_limitations: Subject to NACE H2S limits at high temperatures (typically max 1.5 psi H2S partial pressure).
steel_grades.common_failure_modes: Makeup galling damage, chloride SCC if NACE limits are exceeded.

steel_grades.name: Inconel 625 (Nickel CRA)
steel_grades.description: Non-magnetic nickel-based CRA with excellent strength, toughness, and chemical resistance.
steel_grades.sour_service_suitability: Outstanding (Type 4d nickel-based alloy under NACE MR0175/ISO 15156; virtually immune to H2S limits up to 232°C)
typical_applications: Subsurface safety-critical valve components and springs
Severely corrosive downhole production tubing and casing
steel_grades.mechanical_properties: Yield: 60,000 - 120,000 psi (414 - 827 MPa), Tensile: Min 120,000 psi (827 MPa). Hardness: Max 35 HRC.
steel_grades.h2s_compatibility: Outstanding. Immunity to SSC across all H2S concentrations under NACE MR0175.
steel_grades.co2_compatibility: Exceptional. No weight-loss corrosion in wet CO2.
steel_grades.chloride_resistance: Extreme resistance to chloride pitting, crevice corrosion, and SCC.
steel_grades.temperature_envelope: Excellent stability from cryogenic up to 300°C+.
steel_grades.collapse_yield_considerations: Excellent load limits in cold-worked status; highly ductile.
steel_grades.galling_tendency: Very high galling risk. Specialized premium compounds mandatory.
steel_grades.corrosion_mechanisms: Virtually immune to common downhole corrosion mechanisms.
steel_grades.field_limitations: Very high cost limit; complex machining requirements.

steel_grades.name: Inconel 725 (High-Strength CRA)
steel_grades.description: Age-hardenable nickel-chromium superalloy that combines the corrosion resistance of Alloy 625 with much higher mechanical strength.
usedInEquipment: Shear pins and polished bore receptacles (PBR)
advantages: Extremely high yield strength (>120 ksi) achieved through age hardening
Maintains high impact strength and ductility
limitations: Premium pricing
Requires sophisticated vacuum-induction melting and heat treatment controls
applications: Ultra-HPHT completions in deepwater offshore wells
steel_grades.sour_service_suitability: Excellent, fully approved for severe sour service without H2S limit up to 232C
steel_grades.corrosion_resistance: Equivalent to Inconel 625, but with mechanical envelope capable of exceeding standard yield thresholds.
why_selected: Mandatory when both extreme tensile capacity and maximum corrosion immunity are required
Avoids the weld cladding limitations of Alloy 625
why_avoided: High cost prohibits bulk casing/tubing runs; limited to critical downhole tools and flow controls

steel_grades.name: J55 Carbon Steel
steel_grades.description: API Spec 5CT J55 standard carbon steel grade for shallow, low-pressure tubulars.
steel_grades.mechanical_properties: Yield: 55,000 - 80,000 psi (379 - 552 MPa), Tensile: Min 75,000 psi (517 MPa). Hardness: Max 22 HRC.
steel_grades.h2s_compatibility: Not recommended for sour service. Highly vulnerable to Sulfide Stress Cracking (SSC).
steel_grades.co2_compatibility: Low resistance. Requires continuous corrosion inhibitor injection in sweet CO2 wells.
steel_grades.chloride_resistance: Moderate in low-temperature fresh/salt waters. Susceptible to pitting.
steel_grades.collapse_yield_considerations: Relatively low collapse resistance; limited thickness options compared to L80.
steel_grades.galling_tendency: Low galling risk during makeup. Resilient to standard assembly.
steel_grades.corrosion_mechanisms: General sweet corrosion, galvanic corrosion, and oxygen-driven pitting.
steel_grades.field_limitations: Forbidden in H2S conditions and high-stress HPHT zones.
steel_grades.common_failure_modes: Tubular collapse, burst, general weight-loss corrosion.

steel_grades.name: K55 Carbon Steel
steel_grades.description: API Spec 5CT grade K55 steel, similar to J55 but with higher tensile strength requirements.
steel_grades.mechanical_properties: Yield: 55,000 - 80,000 psi (379 - 552 MPa), Tensile: Min 95,000 psi (655 MPa). Hardness: Max 22 HRC.
steel_grades.h2s_compatibility: Poor. Susceptible to SSC. Not compliant with NACE MR0175.
steel_grades.co2_compatibility: Low. Suffers severe sweet weight-loss corrosion in presence of wet carbon dioxide.
steel_grades.collapse_yield_considerations: Standard collapse ratings for intermediate and surface casing.
steel_grades.galling_tendency: Low galling tendency under standard API thread lubricants.
steel_grades.field_limitations: Do not use in sour service or HPHT reservoir sections.

steel_grades.name: N80 Type 1 Steel
steel_grades.description: API Spec 5CT Grade N80 Type 1 normalized carbon steel for medium-depth wells.
steel_grades.mechanical_properties: Yield: 80,000 - 110,000 psi (552 - 758 MPa), Tensile: Min 100,000 psi (689 MPa). Hardness: Max 28 HRC.
steel_grades.h2s_compatibility: Poor. High hardness normalized-only structure is highly susceptible to SSC.
steel_grades.co2_compatibility: Low. Requires corrosion inhibitor and amine protection.
steel_grades.chloride_resistance: Low. Prone to pitting in high chloride brines.
steel_grades.collapse_yield_considerations: Good intermediate strength casing option for vertical wells.
steel_grades.galling_tendency: Low galling tendency with API compound.
steel_grades.corrosion_mechanisms: Carbon dioxide corrosion, Sulfide Stress Cracking if trace H2S is present.
steel_grades.field_limitations: Strictly sweet service. Not to be used where H2S partial pressure exceeds 0.05 psi.
steel_grades.common_failure_modes: Sulfide stress cracking, sweet corrosion scaling.

steel_grades.name: N80Q Carbon Steel
steel_grades.description: API Spec 5CT Grade N80Q quenched and tempered carbon steel with improved grain structure.
steel_grades.mechanical_properties: Yield: 80,000 - 110,000 psi (552 - 758 MPa), Tensile: Min 100,000 psi (689 MPa). Hardness: Max 25 HRC.
steel_grades.h2s_compatibility: Moderate. Quenched & Tempered structure offers better SSC resistance than Type 1 but still not fully sour-rated.
steel_grades.collapse_yield_considerations: Higher collapse performance than normalized Type 1 due to quenching structure.
steel_grades.corrosion_mechanisms: General sweet corrosion, pitting in chlorides.
steel_grades.field_limitations: Should not be used in critical sour service (H2S concentration > 50 ppm).
steel_grades.common_failure_modes: Acid-driven weight loss, pitting under deposits.

steel_grades.name: L80 Type 1 Carbon Steel
steel_grades.description: API Spec 5CT Grade L80 Type 1 quenched and tempered carbon steel with strictly controlled maximum hardness for H2S service.
steel_grades.mechanical_properties: Yield: 80,000 - 95,000 psi (552 - 655 MPa), Tensile: Min 95,000 psi (655 MPa). Hardness: strictly Max 23 HRC.
steel_grades.h2s_compatibility: NACE MR0175 compliant. Excellent SSC resistance in sour wells.
steel_grades.co2_compatibility: Low resistance. Requires continuous chemical inhibitor injection to prevent weight loss.
steel_grades.chloride_resistance: Moderate. Pitting risk in high temperature, oxygenated brines.
steel_grades.collapse_yield_considerations: Controlled yield strength limits the absolute mechanical loads but ensures ductility.
steel_grades.galling_tendency: Low galling tendency with standard dopes.
steel_grades.corrosion_mechanisms: CO2 weight-loss corrosion, galvanic corrosion in dual completions.
steel_grades.field_limitations: Requires chemical inhibition program for wet CO2 or high-cut water environments.
steel_grades.common_failure_modes: Inhibitor deficiency corrosion, mechanical wear from rod pumping.

steel_grades.name: L80 13Cr Martensitic Steel
steel_grades.description: API Spec 5CT Grade L80 13Cr martensitic stainless steel for wet CO2 applications.
steel_grades.mechanical_properties: Yield: 80,000 - 95,000 psi (552 - 655 MPa), Tensile: Min 95,000 psi (655 MPa). Hardness: Max 23 HRC.
steel_grades.h2s_compatibility: Limited sour service rating. Partial pressure of H2S must be < 0.1 psi (1.5 kPa).
steel_grades.co2_compatibility: Excellent. Highly resistant to sweet weight-loss corrosion up to 150°C.
steel_grades.chloride_resistance: Moderate. Susceptible to pitting and SCC at high chloride concentrations and high temperatures.
steel_grades.collapse_yield_considerations: Identical yield envelope to L80 Carbon Steel but provides stainless benefits.
steel_grades.galling_tendency: High. Requires strict makeup protocols and premium compounds to prevent thread galling.
steel_grades.corrosion_mechanisms: Chloride-induced pitting, Sulfide Stress Cracking if H2S limits are exceeded.
steel_grades.field_limitations: Do not use in highly sour environments or high salinity/high temperature water zones.
steel_grades.common_failure_modes: Thread galling during installation, chloride pitting corrosion.

steel_grades.name: C90 Sour Service Steel
steel_grades.description: API Spec 5CT high-strength sour service grade with strictly controlled martensitic structure and maximum hardness constraint.
steel_grades.mechanical_properties: Yield: 90,000 - 105,000 psi (621 - 724 MPa), Tensile: Min 100,000 psi (689 MPa). Hardness: strictly Max 25.4 HRC.
steel_grades.h2s_compatibility: Fully rated for H2S service under NACE MR0175 limits (Region 3). High SSC resistance.
steel_grades.co2_compatibility: Low. Requires chemical inhibition for wet CO2.
steel_grades.chloride_resistance: Moderate. Standard carbon steel chloride pitting susceptibility.
steel_grades.collapse_yield_considerations: Improved collapse and yield rating over L80, allowing deep sour completion designs.
steel_grades.field_limitations: Requires continuous corrosion inhibition for sweet wet gas.

steel_grades.name: T95 Sour Service Steel
steel_grades.description: High-strength sour service steel grade with strict manufacturing quality control and maximum hardness restriction.
steel_grades.mechanical_properties: Yield: 95,000 - 110,000 psi (655 - 758 MPa), Tensile: Min 105,000 psi (724 MPa). Hardness: strictly Max 26 HRC.
steel_grades.h2s_compatibility: Excellent. Fully sour-service rated (NACE MR0175 Region 3). Outstanding SSC resistance.
steel_grades.co2_compatibility: Low. Susceptible to sweet weight-loss corrosion; requires active chemical inhibition.
steel_grades.collapse_yield_considerations: Very high yield limits allow deep, high-pressure sour well designs.
steel_grades.galling_tendency: Low galling risk with standard API compounds.
steel_grades.corrosion_mechanisms: Weight loss sweet corrosion, pitting in stagnant water phases.
steel_grades.field_limitations: Unprotected carbon steel weight loss occurs in wet carbon dioxide conditions.

steel_grades.name: VM125HC High-Collapse Steel
steel_grades.description: OEM proprietary ultra-high strength, high-collapse casing for extreme depth sweet reservoirs.
steel_grades.mechanical_properties: Yield: 125,000 - 150,000 psi (862 - 1034 MPa), Tensile: Min 135,000 psi (931 MPa). High collapse certified.
steel_grades.h2s_compatibility: Strictly sweet service. Extremely susceptible to rapid SSC due to high yield stress structure.
steel_grades.co2_compatibility: Low. Requires continuous chemical inhibition in wet CO2.
steel_grades.chloride_resistance: Low. Prone to pitting in saline drilling muds.
steel_grades.collapse_yield_considerations: Engineered for high collapse load under heavy salt flow and casing squeezing.
steel_grades.corrosion_mechanisms: General sweet corrosion, hydrogen embrittlement if exposed to acid/H2S.
steel_grades.field_limitations: Strictly prohibited in sour services. Requires careful mud weight design.
steel_grades.common_failure_modes: Accidental H2S exposure SSC failure, installation wear.

steel_grades.name: Incoloy 825 (Nickel-Iron-Chromium CRA)
steel_grades.description: Nickel-iron-chromium alloy with additions of molybdenum and copper, offering excellent resistance to both reducing and oxidizing acids.
steel_grades.sour_service_suitability: Exceptional (Type 4c alloy under NACE MR0175/ISO 15156; no H2S limit up to 132°C)
typical_applications: HPHT sour wells completion and production tubing
steel_grades.mechanical_properties: Yield: 45,000 - 110,000 psi (310 - 758 MPa), Tensile: Min 85,000 psi (586 MPa). Hardness: Max 28 HRC.
steel_grades.h2s_compatibility: Excellent. Widely used for sour gas completions under NACE MR0175.
steel_grades.co2_compatibility: Exceptional. Highly resistant to sweet weight-loss corrosion up to 200°C.
steel_grades.chloride_resistance: High resistance to chloride stress corrosion cracking and pitting.
steel_grades.collapse_yield_considerations: Good yield envelopes in cold-worked high-strength OCTG forms.
steel_grades.galling_tendency: High. Requires premium thread compounds and specialized torque control.
steel_grades.corrosion_mechanisms: Pitting in highly concentrated stagnant acidizing fluids.
steel_grades.field_limitations: Upper temperature limits in severe acid environments.
steel_grades.common_failure_modes: Thread galling, pitting under severe acidizing without flush.

steel_grades.name: Monel 400 (Nickel-Copper Alloy)
steel_grades.description: Nickel-copper alloy with high strength and excellent corrosion resistance in hydrofluoric and sulfuric acids and sea water.
steel_grades.mechanical_properties: Yield: 40,000 - 90,000 psi (276 - 621 MPa), Tensile: Min 80,000 psi (552 MPa). Hardness: Max 23 HRC.
steel_grades.h2s_compatibility: Good. Suitable for sour service but nickel-copper has strict stress limits in presence of H2S.
steel_grades.co2_compatibility: Excellent. Immune to sweet weight-loss corrosion.
steel_grades.chloride_resistance: Outstanding. Excellent resistance to chloride stress corrosion cracking.
steel_grades.collapse_yield_considerations: Lower yield strength compared to Inconel, requiring thicker wall tubing design.
steel_grades.galling_tendency: Moderate to high. Requires standard thread lubricants.
steel_grades.corrosion_mechanisms: Slight oxidation in aerated environments, stress corrosion in hydrofluoric acid vapor.
steel_grades.field_limitations: Not suitable for high-strength casing due to lower yield envelope.

steel_grades.name: Super 13Cr Steel (HP2-13Cr / HP13Cr)
steel_grades.description: High-strength martensitic stainless steel alloyed with Nickel and Molybdenum for wet CO2 and mild H2S service.
steel_grades.chemical_composition: Martensitic stainless steel (13% Cr, 4-6% Ni, 1-2% Mo, ultra-low Carbon)
steel_grades.sour_service_suitability: Resistant to H2S under NACE MR0175 / ISO 15156 limits (pH > 4.5, mild H2S)
steel_grades.temperature_suitability: Thermally stable and highly resistant up to 180C (356F)
steel_grades.corrosion_resistance: Outstanding resistance to wet CO2 and pitting corrosion. Far superior to standard 13Cr.
advantages: Vastly superior CO2 resistance compared to standard 13Cr
Higher yield strength limits allowing deeper completions
Eliminates need for continuous chemical corrosion inhibition
limitations: Significantly higher cost than standard 13Cr
Susceptible to sulfide stress cracking in high H2S concentrations with low pH
steel_grades.engineering_notes: Ensure pH and chloride limits are verified against ISO 15156-3 environmental limits before deployment.

steel_grades.name: C110 Sour Service Steel
steel_grades.description: API 5CT high-strength carbon steel grade with strictly controlled chemistry and hardness for severe H2S sour service.
steel_grades.chemical_composition: Chromium-molybdenum low-alloy steel, controlled hardness (Max 26 HRC)
steel_grades.sour_service_suitability: Fully rated for severe H2S sour service environments under NACE MR0175 / ISO 15156 limits
steel_grades.temperature_suitability: Stable down to -29C, suitable for high-temperature wells up to 200C
steel_grades.corrosion_resistance: Excellent resistance to Sulfide Stress Cracking (SSC) under high tensile loads.
advantages: Highest strength API carbon steel grade rated for severe H2S service
Excellent fracture toughness and crack resistance
Strict quality control ensures minimal hardness variations
limitations: High cost compared to L80 and C90
Requires careful welding and handling procedures to avoid local hardening
steel_grades.engineering_notes: Hardness must be verified across the body and joint to not exceed 26 HRC.

steel_grades.name: Alloy 28 / Sanicro 28
steel_grades.description: Highly alloyed austenitic stainless steel with outstanding resistance to acid stimulation, wet CO2, and severe H2S.
steel_grades.chemical_composition: High-alloy austenitic stainless steel (31% Ni, 27% Cr, 3.5% Mo, 1% Cu)
steel_grades.sour_service_suitability: Outstanding resistance in severe sour environments (up to 100% H2S)
steel_grades.temperature_suitability: Exceptional mechanical and chemical stability up to 200C (392F)
steel_grades.corrosion_resistance: Excellent resistance to localized pitting, crevice corrosion, and chloride stress corrosion cracking.
advantages: Excellent cost-to-performance ratio compared to nickel-based alloys
Immune to chloride-induced stress corrosion cracking
Outstanding resistance in hydrochloric and hydrofluoric acid stimulations
limitations: High thermal expansion coefficient requires careful design considerations
Requires specialized welding procedures
typical_applications: Deep sour gas wells with high chloride content
steel_grades.engineering_notes: Ensure compatibility with completion fluids (e.g. zinc bromides can cause stress corrosion under high temperatures).

steel_grades.name: Titanium Grade 5 (Ti-6Al-4V)
steel_grades.description: High-strength alpha-beta titanium alloy offering exceptional strength-to-weight ratio and superb corrosion resistance in geothermal steam.
steel_grades.chemical_composition: Titanium alloy (6% Aluminum, 4% Vanadium, balance Titanium)
steel_grades.sour_service_suitability: Highly resistant to sour environments and wet acid gases up to high temperatures
steel_grades.temperature_suitability: Retains structural and mechanical properties up to 350C (662F)
steel_grades.corrosion_resistance: Virtually immune to pitting, crevice corrosion, and sweet/sour weight-loss corrosion.
advantages: Exceptional strength-to-weight ratio (45% lighter than steel)
Excellent flexibility and outstanding fatigue life in marine environments
Total corrosion immunity in geothermal steam and wet chlorides
limitations: Extremely high raw material and fabrication cost
High galling tendency during makeup requiring special thread coatings
steel_grades.engineering_notes: Use specialized copper-free, non-metallic thread compounds to prevent electrochemical galvanic corrosion.

failures.name: Sulfide Stress Cracking (SSC / Водородное растрескивание)
failures.description: Catastrophic failure mechanism where high-strength steel becomes brittle and cracks under tensile stress in the presence of H2S and water.
typical_applications: Prevention: Strict usage of L80 / T95 steel grades in sour service.
typical_failures: Sudden parting of tubing string without warning
why_selected: Critical understanding of SSC is required for all sour completions design
why_avoided: Avoid P110 and Q125 steel grades in sour environments unless absolute dry conditions are guaranteed
failures.symptoms: Sudden pressure drop in tubing, tubing string parting (separation), zero plastical deformation at the fracture site (brittle look).
failures.root_causes: Atomic hydrogen absorption into the steel crystal lattice. Hydrogen atoms migrate to stress concentration points, causing lattice lattice distortion and microcracking.
failures.trigger_environments: Presence of H2S (even in ppm levels), liquid water (wet gas or brine), low temperature (<82°C / 180°F), and tensile stress (axial load or internal pressure).
failures.typical_metallurgy: High-strength low-alloy steels (P110, Q125) and weld heat-affected zones with hardness exceeding 22 HRC.
failures.prevention_methods: Use controlled hardness steels (L80, T95, C90) with max hardness 22 HRC. Inject corrosion inhibitors to form protective film. Optimize string design to reduce tensile stress.
failures.field_troubleshooting: Perform ultrasonic testing of joints. Run caliper logs to detect tubing parting. Check gas analysis for H2S and water dew point.

failures.name: Thread Galling (Задиры резьбы)
failures.description: Severe wear mechanism occurring during connection makeup, where friction causes localized welding and tearing of thread surfaces.
typical_failures: Inability to achieve correct makeup torque
Leaking seal due to deformed metal-to-metal barrier
why_selected: Galling is the number one cause of premium connection rejection during running operations
why_avoided: Avoid rapid makeup speeds and running alloys (like 13Cr) dry without specialized lubricants
failures.symptoms: Makeup torque spikes before reaching shoulder position, connection gets hot, thread profile is physically deformed or metal slivers are visible.
failures.root_causes: High contact pressure and sliding friction strip the oxide film from metal surfaces, allowing direct metal-to-metal contact and micro-welding.
failures.trigger_environments: High makeup rotation speed (>10 RPM), misalignment of joints during stabbing, dirt or debris in threads, and dry threads (no dope).
failures.typical_metallurgy: Corrosion Resistant Alloys (13Cr, Super 13Cr, Duplex, Inconel 718) are highly susceptible due to their protective chromium oxide film.
failures.prevention_methods: Use correct API 5A3 thread dope (or dopeless dry coatings). Ensure perfect vertical alignment using stabbing guides. Control makeup speed (<5 RPM). Use copper plating or thread blasting.
failures.field_troubleshooting: Immediately stop makeup if torque spikes. Break out the connection slowly. Inspect with thread gauges. Minor damage can be repaired using hand files.

failures.name: Hydrogen Induced Cracking (HIC)
failures.description: Stepwise internal cracking in steel occurring without external stress, driven by hydrogen diffusion.
failures.symptoms: Blistering on tubular surfaces, internal laminations and parallel cracks visible in ultrasonic inspection.
failures.root_cause: H2S corrosion produces atomic hydrogen which diffuses into steel and recombines at inclusions (MnS).
failures.trigger_conditions: Presence of wet H2S, low pH, high manganese-sulfide inclusion density.
failures.prevention: Use HIC-resistant steel (controlled sulfur < 0.002%, calcium treatment for shape control).
failures.typical_metallurgy: Low-strength carbon steels (e.g. J55, K55, normalized steels) with high sulfur content.
failures.field_troubleshooting: Perform ultrasonic testing. Replace strings with HIC-resistant or CRA materials.

failures.name: Stress Oriented HIC (SOHIC)
failures.description: Array of HIC cracks stacked perpendicularly to tensile stress, leading to rapid brittle fracture.
failures.symptoms: Sudden brittle failure of casing or tubing under tension, cracks starting from pitting or defects.
failures.root_cause: Interaction of high tensile stress and HIC, aligning hydrogen cracks into a critical fracture path.
failures.trigger_conditions: Wet H2S, high tensile load, susceptible normalized metallurgy.
failures.prevention: Use quenched and tempered low-hardness steels. Maintain design tension safety factors.
failures.field_troubleshooting: Check tensile calculations. Implement chemical wash inhibitors. Replace with sour-service Q&T grade.

failures.name: Sulfide Weight Loss Corrosion
failures.description: General weight loss and pitting corrosion driven by hydrogen sulfide reactions, forming iron sulfide scale.
failures.symptoms: Black powder or scale (FeS) deposits in production fluids, localized pitting under scale.
failures.root_cause: Direct chemical reaction of H2S and iron, forming cathodic iron sulfide scales that drive galvanic attack.
failures.trigger_conditions: Wet H2S gas or liquids, flow turbulence, temperatures above 60°C.
failures.prevention: Use 13Cr or nickel CRA alloys. Continuous injection of film-forming corrosion inhibitors.
failures.field_troubleshooting: Flush well with scale dissolver. Increase inhibitor concentration.

failures.name: CO₂ Corrosion (Sweet Corrosion)
failures.description: Severe weight-loss and mesa-type corrosion in carbon steels due to carbonic acid formation.
failures.symptoms: Mesa-type flat-bottomed pits in steel, rapid wall thinning, iron carbonate scale.
failures.root_cause: Dissolution of CO2 in water forming carbonic acid, which directly attacks iron.
failures.trigger_conditions: High CO2 partial pressure, high water cut, temperatures between 60°C and 120°C.
failures.prevention: Use 13Cr martensitic stainless steel. Inject continuous organic amine inhibitors.
failures.field_troubleshooting: Run caliper survey to detect thin walls. Switch string to 13Cr.

failures.name: Chloride Stress Corrosion Cracking (Chloride SCC)
failures.description: Brittle cracking of stainless steel and CRA alloys in hot chloride solutions under tensile stress.
failures.symptoms: Fine branched cracking visible on CRA surfaces under dye penetrant testing, rapid pressure leak.
failures.root_cause: Synergistic effect of chloride ions, oxygen, high temperature, and tensile stress.
failures.trigger_conditions: Chloride concentration > 1000 ppm, temperature > 60°C, high tensile stress.
failures.prevention: Use Super Duplex or Nickel-based alloys (Inconel 625/718). Limit oxygen ingress.
failures.field_troubleshooting: Analyze water salinity and oxygen levels. Upgrade metallurgy.

failures.name: Pitting Corrosion
failures.description: Highly localized corrosive attack producing deep cavities or holes in passivated alloys.
failures.symptoms: Small pinhole leaks in casing or tubing, localized deep holes with surrounding surface unaffected.
failures.root_cause: Local breakdown of the passive chromium oxide film in presence of chlorides and oxygen.
failures.trigger_conditions: Chloride fluids, presence of oxygen, stagnant fluid conditions.
failures.prevention: Use materials with high PREN (PREN > 40, e.g. Inconel 625). Avoid stagnant fluid phases.
failures.field_troubleshooting: Perform hydrochloric acid wash flush. Maintain flow velocity.

failures.name: Galvanic Corrosion
failures.description: Accelerated corrosion of a less noble metal when in electrical contact with a more noble metal in an electrolyte.
failures.symptoms: Severe localized wall loss on carbon steel components immediately adjacent to stainless steel joints.
failures.root_cause: Potential difference between dissimilar metals creating a galvanic cell.
failures.trigger_conditions: Dissimilar metals connected in conductive completion brine.
failures.prevention: Use dielectric isolation subs or ensure adjacent materials match in electrochemical potential.
failures.field_troubleshooting: Install insulating pup joints. Ensure inhibitor program is active.

failures.name: Fluid Washout
failures.description: Erosion-corrosion channel cut through connection seal or thread due to high-velocity fluid escape.
failures.symptoms: Casing or tubing pressure leak, connection pin or box displaying smooth deep groove cuts.
failures.root_cause: Micro-gap in thread seal allows high-pressure fluid to leak, causing mechanical erosion.
failures.trigger_conditions: Insufficient makeup torque, damaged seal surfaces, abrasive particles in fluid.
failures.prevention: Follow strict torque-turn running procedures. Visually inspect seals before running.
failures.field_troubleshooting: Lay down damaged pipe. Recut threads at shop.

failures.name: Tubular Collapse
failures.description: Structural failure of pipe wall buckling inward due to excessive external pressure.
failures.symptoms: Restriction of wellbore ID, inability to run downhole tools, drop in production.
failures.root_cause: External pressure load exceeds the collapse capacity of the tubular.
failures.trigger_conditions: High mud weight, reservoir subsidence, tectonic salt flow, casing evacuation.
failures.prevention: Use high-collapse rated casing (e.g. VM125HC). Ensure casing is fully cemented.
failures.field_troubleshooting: Run lead impression block or casing caliper. Swage casing or sidetrack.

failures.name: Tubular Burst
failures.description: Tensile failure of pipe wall tearing outward due to excessive internal pressure.
failures.symptoms: Sudden loss of wellhead pressure, gas or oil returning in casing annulus.
failures.root_cause: Internal pressure exceeds the design burst rating of the steel tubular.
failures.trigger_conditions: High-pressure gas kicks, casing pressure buildup (sap), thermal expansion of trapped annular fluid.
failures.prevention: Use high-burst grade steels (P110, Q125). Implement annular pressure management.
failures.field_troubleshooting: Isolate leak zone using packers or scab liners. Cement squeeze.

failures.name: Fatigue Cracking
failures.description: Progressive, localized structural damage occurring when a material is subjected to cyclic loading.
failures.symptoms: Transverse cracks in drill pipe body or tool joints, sudden parting of the drill string.
failures.root_cause: Cyclic bending stresses exceeding fatigue limits, starting from surface defects or pits.
failures.trigger_conditions: Drilling in high dogleg severity intervals, drill string vibration.
failures.prevention: Minimize dogleg severity. Run drill string inspections regularly. Avoid resonance vibration speeds.
failures.field_troubleshooting: Check dogleg survey. Perform electromagnetic inspection of the string.

failures.name: Elastomer Seal Failure
failures.description: Loss of sealing integrity in packer or BOP rubber element, allowing fluid communication.
failures.symptoms: Annular pressure communication, fluid leak at surface BOP stacks.
failures.root_cause: Thermal degradation, chemical attack, or explosive decompression (RGD) cracking.
failures.trigger_conditions: High temperature, sour gas exposure, rapid pressure drops.
failures.prevention: Select chemically inert elastomers (FFKM/Kalrez). Specify RGD-resistant materials.
failures.field_troubleshooting: Run packer leak test. Retrieve completion string and replace seal elements.

failures.name: Thread Jump-Out (Connection Parting)
failures.description: Complete separation of a threaded joint under high tensile or bending load without structural steel fracture.
failures.symptoms: Annular leak, drop in hook load weight, casing string parting.
failures.root_cause: Tensile stress causes the box to expand or the pin to compress, letting threads slip past each other.
failures.trigger_conditions: High tensile load, insufficient thread interference, API round thread connection type.
failures.prevention: Use premium connections with negative load flank angles (e.g. VAM TOP, Tenaris Blue).
failures.field_troubleshooting: Run casing caliper. Run casing patch or back off and fish.

failures.name: Over-Torque Damage
failures.description: Plastic deformation and yielding of connection pin/box due to excessive make-up torque.
failures.symptoms: Difficulty in unscrewing connection, flared box ends, swollen pin shoulders.
failures.root_cause: Applied torque exceeds the torsional yield strength of the connection.
failures.trigger_conditions: Incorrect rig torque calibration, lack of torque-turn monitoring.
failures.prevention: Use strict torque-turn tables. Calibrate rig tongs. Use shoulder-stop connections.
failures.field_troubleshooting: Lay down damaged pipe. Recut threads at qualified machine shop.

failures.name: Thread Connection Back-Off
failures.description: Unintended unscrewing of a threaded connection during downhole rotation or vibration.
failures.symptoms: Parted drill string or casing, loss of torque signature on rig floor.
failures.root_cause: Dynamic vibration and reverse rotation torque overcome thread friction.
failures.trigger_conditions: Drilling with high torque/vibration, reverse rotation, lack of thread locking compound.
usedInEquipment: Casing strings run in horizontal wells
failures.prevention: Apply thread-locking compound (e.g. Bakerlok) on casing connections. Use double-shoulder joints.

failures.name: Erosion (Sand Erosion)
failures.description: Mechanical wear of steel surface caused by the high-velocity impact of solid sand particles in fluid.
failures.symptoms: Wall thinning in elbows, chokes, blast joints, and tubing connections near perforation.
failures.root_cause: High velocity gas/oil containing sand grains grinding down steel walls.
failures.trigger_conditions: Unconsolidated sand reservoirs, high production flow rate, bends in piping.
failures.prevention: Install sand control screens downhole. Limit fluid velocity. Use heavy-wall blast joints.
failures.field_troubleshooting: Reduce choke size. Replace blast joints with erosion-resistant materials.
